data.content:
### Cal Provider Setup in React/Next.js

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/atoms/cal-provider.mdx

This JavaScript snippet demonstrates how to set up the CalProvider at the root of a React or Next.js application. It imports necessary global styles and the CalProvider component. Key props include `clientId`, configuration `options` (like `apiUrl` and `refreshUrl`), and an optional `accessToken` for authenticated user scheduling. Ensure environment variables are correctly set for API URLs.

```javascript
import "@calcom/atoms/globals.min.css";
import { CalProvider } from '@calcom/atoms';

function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) {
  const accessToken = "managed-user-access-token";

  return (
    <CalProvider
      clientId={process.env.CAL_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID ?? ""}
      options={{
        apiUrl: process.env.CAL_API_URL ?? "https://api.cal.com/v2",
        refreshUrl: process.env.REFRESH_URL
      }}
      accessToken={accessToken}
    >
      <Component {...pageProps} />
    </CalProvider>
  );
}

export default MyApp;
```

--------------------------------

### Configure CalProvider with Advanced Props

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/quickstart.mdx

This code demonstrates advanced configuration of the CalProvider component in a Next.js/React app. It includes setting the language to French ('fr'), overriding labels, disabling automatic timezone updates, providing a timezone change callback, and passing custom children. This allows for fine-grained control over the Cal.com atoms' behavior and presentation.

```javascript
import "@calcom/atoms/globals.min.css";
import { CalProvider } from '@calcom/atoms';
import { useState, useEffect } from 'react';

function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) {
  const [accessToken, setAccessToken] = useState("");

  useEffect(() => {
    fetch(`/api/users/${pageProps.userId}`, {
    }).then(async (res) => {
      const data = await res.json();
      setAccessToken(data.accessToken);
    });
  }, []);

  return (
    <CalProvider
      accessToken={accessToken}
      clientId={process.env.CAL_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID ?? ""}
      options={{
        apiUrl: process.env.CAL_API_URL ?? "",
        refreshUrl: "/api/refresh"
      }}
      language="fr"
      labels={{
        date_overrides: "Omlette du Fromage!"
      }}
      autoUpdateTimezone={false}
      onTimezoneChange={() => {
        console.log("Timezone changed successfully")
      }}
      children={<>This is the child component</>}
    >
      <Component {...pageProps} />
    </CalProvider>
  );
}

export default MyApp;
```

--------------------------------

### SSO Setup - SAML

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/guides/auth-and-provision/sso-setup.mdx

Instructions for configuring SAML-based Single Sign-On (SSO) with Cal.com. This involves creating a SAML application with your Identity Provider (IdP), configuring access, retrieving metadata, and then configuring SSO within Cal.com.

```APIDOC
## Setting up SAML login

### Description
Follow these steps to set up SAML login for your Cal.com organization.

### Method
Not Applicable (Configuration Steps)

### Endpoint
`settings/organizations/sso`

### Parameters
N/A

### Request Example
N/A

### Response
N/A

## SAML Registration with Identity Providers

### Description
This section details the specific settings required to configure SAML with your Identity Provider (IdP) and obtain the necessary XML metadata for Cal.com.

### Method
Not Applicable (Configuration Details)

### Endpoint
N/A

### Parameters
#### SAML Settings
- **Assertion consumer service URL / Single Sign-On URL / Destination URL** (string) - Required - `https://app.cal.com/api/saml/callback`
- **Entity ID / Identifier / Audience URI / Audience Restriction** (string) - Required - `https://saml.cal.com`
- **Response** (string) - Required - `Signed`
- **Assertion Signature** (string) - Required - `Signed`
- **Signature Algorithm** (string) - Required - `RSA-SHA256`
- **Assertion Encryption** (string) - Required - `Unencrypted`
- **Name ID Format** (string) - Required - `EmailAddress`
- **Application username** (string) - Required - `email`

#### Mapping Attributes / Attribute Statements

| Name      | Name Format | Value          |
|:----------|:------------|:---------------|
| firstName | Basic       | user.firstName |
| lastName  | Basic       | user.lastName  |
```

--------------------------------

### Self-Hosted with Billing Configuration (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/ee/organizations/lib/service/onboarding/OrganizationOnboardingSetup.md

This configuration enables optional billing on self-hosted Cal.com instances by forcing hosted features and providing Stripe credentials. This ensures all users will use the billing-enabled onboarding service.

```bash
# Domain: custom.example.com
NEXT_PUBLIC_HOSTED_CAL_FEATURES=true
STRIPE_CLIENT_ID=ca_xxx
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY=pk_live_xxx
STRIPE_PRIVATE_KEY=sk_live_xxx
```

--------------------------------

### Hosted Cal.com Configuration (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/ee/organizations/lib/service/onboarding/OrganizationOnboardingSetup.md

This configuration represents the official Cal.com hosted environment. It includes Stripe credentials and results in team billing being enabled for all users.

```bash
# Domain: *.cal.com, *.cal.dev, etc.
STRIPE_CLIENT_ID=ca_xxx
NEXT_PUBLIC_STRIPE_PUBLIC_KEY=pk_live_xxx
STRIPE_PRIVATE_KEY=sk_live_xxx
```

--------------------------------

### E2E Testing Configuration (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/ee/organizations/lib/service/onboarding/OrganizationOnboardingSetup.md

This configuration is used for end-to-end testing in Cal.com. By setting `NEXT_PUBLIC_IS_E2E=1`, the billing flow is completely bypassed, and all users will utilize the self-hosted onboarding service.

```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_IS_E2E=1
# Any other configuration
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Environment Variables for Cal.com Organizations

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/guides/organization/organization-setup.mdx

Set essential environment variables in your .env file to enable and configure the Cal.com organizations feature. These variables control application URLs, authentication, and the enablement of organizations.

```bash
NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBAPP_URL=http://app.cal.local:3000
NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBSITE_URL=http://app.cal.local:3000
NEXTAUTH_URL=http://app.cal.local:3000
ORGANIZATIONS_ENABLED=1
```

--------------------------------

### Configure PostgreSQL Database URL

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/local-development.mdx

Sets the `DATABASE_URL` environment variable in the `.env` file for PostgreSQL connection. Replace placeholders with actual credentials and host information.

```bash
DATABASE_URL='postgresql://<user>:<pass>@<db-host>:<db-port>'
```

--------------------------------

### Configure API V1 Port on Windows

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/local-development.mdx

Modifies the 'dev' script in `apps/api/package.json` to explicitly set the PORT to 3003 for Windows users. This ensures the API V1 server starts on the correct port.

```json
"dev": "set PORT=3003 && next dev"
```

--------------------------------

### API Configuration (.env) for Cal.com Examples

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/platform/examples/base/README.md

This snippet shows the necessary environment variables to configure the Cal.com API V2 for local development. Ensure you replace placeholders like NEXTAUTH_SECRET with actual values from your root .env file. These variables control the API's operational parameters, including database connections and security.

```env
NODE_ENV="development"
API_PORT=5555
DATABASE_READ_URL="postgresql://postgres:@localhost:5450/calendso"
DATABASE_WRITE_URL="postgresql://postgres:@localhost:5450/calendso"
API_URL="http://localhost"
NEXTAUTH_SECRET="copy from .env of root of repository cloned in step 1"
DATABASE_URL="postgresql://postgres:@localhost:5450/calendso"
JWT_SECRET="asjdijI1JIO12I3O89198jojioSAJDU"
REDIS_URL="redis://localhost:6379"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Local DNS for Cal.com Organizations

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/self-hosting/guides/organization/organization-setup.mdx

Modify your system's hosts file to map local development domains to your local machine. This is crucial for Cal.com organizations to function correctly in a self-hosted environment, allowing subdomains like 'app.cal.local' to resolve.

```bash
127.0.0.1 app.cal.local
```

--------------------------------

### Self-Hosted without Billing Configuration (Bash)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/ee/organizations/lib/service/onboarding/OrganizationOnboardingSetup.md

This is the default configuration for self-hosted Cal.com instances without Stripe integration. Billing is disabled, and the onboarding flow depends on user roles: admins use the self-hosted service, while regular users are directed to the billing service (which may fail).

```bash
# Domain: custom.example.com
# No Stripe configuration
```

--------------------------------

### Initialize CalProvider in Next.js/React App

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/platform/quickstart.mdx

This code initializes the CalProvider component, which is essential for providing necessary information to Cal.com atoms. It requires client ID and API options, such as the API URL and refresh URL, typically fetched from environment variables. Wrapping the application with CalProvider enables its components to function correctly.

```javascript
import "@calcom/atoms/globals.min.css";
import { CalProvider } from '@calcom/atoms';

function MyApp({ Component, pageProps }) {
  return (
    <CalProvider
      clientId={process.env.CAL_OAUTH_CLIENT_ID ?? ""}
      options={{
        apiUrl: process.env.CAL_API_URL ?? "",
        refreshUrl: process.env.REFRESH_URL
      }}
    >
      <Component {...pageProps} />
    </CalProvider>
  );
}

export default MyApp;
```

--------------------------------

### SSO Setup - OIDC

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/guides/auth-and-provision/sso-setup.mdx

Instructions for configuring OpenID Connect (OIDC) based Single Sign-On (SSO) with Cal.com. This involves gathering OIDC credentials, accessing Cal.com's SSO settings, and inputting the required information.

```APIDOC
## Setting up OIDC login

### Description
Follow these steps to set up OIDC login for your Cal.com organization.

### Method
Not Applicable (Configuration Steps)

### Endpoint
`settings/organizations/sso`

### Parameters
#### Request Body Fields for OIDC Configuration
- **Client Secret** (string) - Required - Your OIDC provider's client secret.
- **Client ID** (string) - Required - Your OIDC provider's client ID.
- **Well Known URL** (string) - Required - The OIDC provider's well-known configuration URL.
```

--------------------------------

### Deploy Prisma Database

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/local-development.mdx

Applies the database schema defined in `packages/prisma/schema.prisma` to the configured database using Prisma.

```bash
yarn workspace @calcom/prisma db-deploy
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Environment Variables for Organizations

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/packages/features/ee/organizations/README.md

This section outlines the necessary environment variables for enabling the Cal.com Organizations feature. These variables control licensing, web app URL, authentication, and optional features like auto-linking users to organizations.

```bash
export CALCOM_LICENSE_KEY="your_license_key"
export NEXT_PUBLIC_WEBAPP_URL="http://app.cal.local:3000"
export NEXTAUTH_URL="http://app.cal.local:3000"
export NEXTAUTH_COOKIE_DOMAIN=".cal.local"
export ORGANIZATIONS_ENABLED="1"
export STRIPE_ORG_MONTHLY_PRICE_ID="your_stripe_price_id"
# Optional:
# export ORGANIZATIONS_AUTOLINK="1"
```

--------------------------------

### Configure Cal.com Email Testing with Mailtrap (Nodemailer)

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/guides/email/setup-mailtrap-for-email-testing.mdx

This configuration sets up Cal.com to use Mailtrap for email testing via Nodemailer. It requires updating environment variables with Mailtrap's SMTP server details, including host, port, username, and password.

```env
EMAIL_SERVER_HOST='sandbox.smtp.mailtrap.io'
EMAIL_SERVER_PORT=2525
EMAIL_SERVER_USER='XXX'
EMAIL_SERVER_PASSWORD='XXX'
```

```env
EMAIL_FROM='notifications@yourselfhostedcal.com'
```

--------------------------------

### Copy .env Files for Cal.com API

Source: https://github.com/calcom/cal.com/blob/main/docs/developing/local-development.mdx

Copies example environment files to their active counterparts for API v1 and v2, and the root .env file. This step is crucial for configuring API settings.

```bash
cp apps/api/.env.example apps/api/{version}/.env
cp .env.example .env
```
